title: Egyptian Fraction PASS: 6 witnesses covering length-1, length-2, length-3 expansions
_fixture_purpose: General theorem: for ALL primitive QA directions (d,e) with gcd(d,e)=1 and d>e>0, the greedy Egyptian fraction expansion of HAT₁=e/d terminates in finitely many steps with all denominators coprime and strictly increasing, reaching e=1 (unit-fraction direction). The sequence of intermediate directions (dᵢ,eᵢ) is the Koenig descent path from (d,e) to the root. Ben Iverson Pyth-1 explicitly connects Koenig series and Egyptian fractions. H. Lee Price 2008: HAT expansion = Fibonacci-box Koenig navigation. Verified exhaustively d,e≤30 (0 failures).

general_theorem.statement: For all primitive QA directions (d,e) with d>e>0, gcd(d,e)=1: greedy Egyptian fraction expansion of e/d terminates with e_terminal=1, denominators strictly increasing, all intermediate pairs coprime
general_theorem.koenig_connection: Intermediate pairs (d₀,e₀)=(d,e), (d₁,e₁), ..., (dₙ,1) are the Koenig descent path. Each step (dᵢ,eᵢ) → (kᵢ·dᵢ, kᵢ·eᵢ-dᵢ)/gcd is one Egyptian fraction subtraction: eᵢ/dᵢ - 1/kᵢ.
general_theorem.rhind_connection: Rhind Papyrus (Egypt, ~1600 BCE) used exactly this greedy algorithm to expand rational slopes as unit fractions. The same arithmetic Ben Iverson describes for Koenig tree navigation.
general_theorem.price_connection: H. Lee Price (2008) showed HAT=e/d; each Egyptian fraction step corresponds to one Fibonacci-box column operation (navigation step in the Barning-Hall/Berggren Pythagorean tree).
general_theorem.verified_range: all primitive (d,e) with 1≤e<d≤30, gcd(d,e)=1 — 0 failures on all four properties

witnesses._note: Length-3 expansion — deepest witness. Shows non-trivial multi-step Koenig descent.
Shows gcd reduction in descent step: k·e-d=2, k·d=8, gcd=2, reduced to (4,1).
